events.title: Israel Launches Airstrikes in Southern Lebanon
events.desc: Israel conducted airstrikes across southern Lebanon, targeting areas around the city of Nabatieh and over twenty other locations. This action follows intensified fighting between Israel and Hezbollah and occurs amidst ongoing diplomatic efforts to de-escalate the conflict. The strikes indicate a continued escalation of hostilities in the region.

events.title: US President Trump Claims US-Iran Deal Imminent
events.desc: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that a deal with Iran to end the ongoing conflict was scheduled to be signed on Sunday, June 14, 2026. He further stated that the Strait of Hormuz would be immediately reopened to all traffic post-signing. This statement comes amid a complex diplomatic landscape with varying timelines reported by different parties.

events.title: Iran Disputes Immediate US-Iran Deal Signing
events.desc: Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson, Esmaeil Baghaei, stated that the signing of the Islamabad memorandum would not occur on Sunday, June 14, 2026. While not ruling out a signing in the coming days, he cautioned that hesitation from the other side required careful consideration of any comments regarding the date.

events.title: UK Prime Minister Discusses Iran Conflict Resolution with US President
events.desc: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer spoke with U.S. President Donald Trump regarding efforts to end the Iran conflict. Starmer expressed support for the progress made and reiterated Britain's readiness to assist in implementing any peace agreement, emphasizing the need for international cooperation. Both leaders also agreed on restoring freedom of navigation.

events.title: Iran Claims Attacks on US Assets in Kuwait, Jordan, Bahrain
events.desc: Iran alleged that it responded to prior US strikes by targeting American assets in Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain. Bahrain reported damage to its capital city overnight following these alleged retaliatory actions.
events.location: Kuwait City, Kuwait / Amman, Jordan / Manama, Bahrain

events.title: US Strikes Iranian Oil Tanker, Killing Three Indian Sailors
events.desc: The US military reportedly struck a Palau-flagged oil tanker on Wednesday, June 11, 2026, after accusing it of not complying with directions. The strike resulted in the deaths of three Indian sailors onboard.

events.title: Israeli Strikes in Southern Lebanon Kill 17
events.desc: Seventeen individuals were reportedly killed in Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon. Nine fatalities occurred in a series of attacks in the town of Tayr Debba, according to Lebanon's state news agency.

events.title: US Strikes Iran in Response to Helicopter Downing
events.desc: President Donald Trump accused Iran of shooting down a US military helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz and vowed a response, which was subsequently carried out. The strike signifies a direct kinetic exchange between US and Iranian forces.

events.title: Hezbollah Fires at Israel, Prompting Israeli Strikes on Beirut
events.desc: Israel carried out strikes on a Beirut suburb, stating they were in response to Hezbollah firing at Israeli territory. This indicates a continuation of cross-border hostilities despite a recent US-brokered truce.

events.title: One Dead, Five Injured in Shooting Incident in Israel
events.desc: A shooting incident occurred in Israel where a suspect opened fire at multiple locations from a vehicle, resulting in one death and five injuries. The suspect was subsequently shot and killed by authorities.

events.title: Iran and Israel Agree to Pause Strikes, Warn of Retaliation
events.desc: Both Iran and Israel announced a pause in their military strikes, with Israel's Prime Minister stating the country is holding fire "at the moment." Iran's armed forces confirmed they had stopped military action. However, both sides issued warnings of retaliation if the ceasefire is breached again.

events.title: Funeral Held for Baby Killed by Israeli Troops
events.desc: A funeral was held for a seven-month-old baby who was reportedly shot and killed by Israeli troops in the occupied West Bank. The infant's body was carried by his father to the graveyard, wrapped in a Palestinian flag.

events.title: Russian Grain Smuggling to Libya Tracked via AIS Technique
events.desc: A new technique using AIS data has helped track the expansion of Russian grain smuggling, with a specific incident involving the bulk carrier Grumant stocking up on grain at the occupied Ukrainian Port of Feodosia before a two-month journey to the Port of Benghazi in Libya.

events.title: Iranian Police Infrastructure Damaged in Attacks
events.desc: Bellingcat identified at least 80 police stations or infrastructure related to law enforcement agencies and the Basij paramilitary group that have been damaged or destroyed in the initial three weeks of the conflict involving the United States and Israel against Iran. Experts suggest the aim is to degrade the Iranian regime's "repressive capacity."

events.title: US and Iran Exchange Strikes Across Middle East
events.desc: The US and Iran engaged in kinetic exchanges across the Middle East for a second consecutive day. Iran stated it responded to US strikes by targeting American assets in Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain.
